source.catalog_ref: HV24 (user-assigned national aerospace student benchmark + BNL MagLev collegiate/HS class)
source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt describing a central spine, two magnet-carrier rails, a payload cradle, and two bumper-stop plates; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the national aerospace student benchmark 2026 Tech-Demo + Brookhaven Lab MagLev Competition 2026 HS-class passive-magnet levitation vehicle chassis as a multi-part assembly, explicitly modeling the 6061-T6 aluminum chassis as the physical collection of separately-manufactured billet-machined components that are joined (screw-bonded, bolted, or press-fit) to form the small-vehicle platform. The original prompt treats the chassis as monolithic ('single billet-machined 6061-T6 part, no welds, no bolted-on tabs'); this multipart decomposition exposes the same structure as its constituent part families so the assembly-level interface verification can be exercised. A monolithic manufacturing path remains permissible but the CAD and FEA must be composed part-by-part for multipart verification.

The assembly consists of five part families: (A) the central spine, a 400 mm long x 60 mm wide x 20 mm tall billet that forms the main structural backbone; (B) two magnet-carrier rails running along the underside of the spine, each 400 mm long x 20 mm wide x 4 mm thick with an 18 mm wide x 5 mm deep channel that seats 20 N42 ceramic magnets at 20 mm pitch; (C) the integral payload cradle on top of the spine, a 100 mm long x 40 mm wide x 10 mm deep trough with 4 M4 tie-down clearance holes; (D) two bumper-stop plates at the front and rear, each 30 mm wide x 15 mm tall x 4 mm thick vertical plate; (E) the tie-down and interface fasteners (4 M4 for payload tie-down, plus the short stub screws that tie the rails and bumpers to the spine in a multipart build).

Geometry inherits the original sample verbatim: spine 400x60x20 mm, two magnet-carrier rails 400x20x4 with 18x5 channel at 25 mm rail gap, payload cradle 100x40x10 with 4x 4.5 mm holes, bumpers 30x15x4 at both ends, overall bounding envelope 400 x 60 x 30 mm.

Material inherits verbatim: 6061-T6 aluminum for all primary parts (E 68.9 GPa, nu 0.33, density 2700 kg/m^3, yield 276 MPa, UTS 310 MPa, endurance 97 MPa at 5e8 cycles). Fasteners are M4 grade-8.8 steel (standard for small-vehicle tie-downs; not specified by the original sample).

Loads inherit verbatim: LC1 peak-descent run-out at the end of the 16 ft / 0.61 m drop track with 0.47 kg total vehicle mass, 3.46 m/s peak velocity, 3.5 N longitudinal + 2.3 N lateral at the bumpers with magnet-carrier rails constrained by distributed levitation forces; LC2 end-of-track bumper hit with 25 N impulse over 10 ms on the rear bumper (2.8 J absorbed by elastomer-bumper pad modeled as stiffness BC); LC3 0.3 m install drop shock with 553 N vertical on the payload cradle (elastomer pad on bench with 5 mm compression), chassis inverted constrained at bumpers.

Pass/fail criteria inherit verbatim from the original and apply at the assembly level: (R1) peak von Mises under LC1 <= 138 MPa (yield/2.0); (R2) peak von Mises under LC2/LC3 <= 184 MPa (yield/1.5 transient); (R3) first six natural frequencies (chassis + magnets + payload lumped mass) >= 40 Hz; (R4) chassis mass <= 0.35 kg; (R5) magnet-carrier rail channel flatness deviation under LC1 <= 0.2 mm; (R6) overall vehicle length <= 406 mm (BNL <=16 in). Added assembly-level targets under fem_check_policy.assembled_only: connection DCR at rail-spine, bumper-spine, and payload-spine interfaces must be reported; per-part stresses are reported for diagnostic purposes but do not gate pass/fail.

Thermal, fluid, buckling, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 of the assembly with each body (spine, each rail, each bumper, the payload cradle) as a distinct solid tagged with part_family (A..E) and instance_index, plus a report giving max von Mises per case, first six frequencies, rail-channel flatness deviation under LC1, vehicle length, assembly mass, and connection DCR at each interface.
short_prompt:
Design a TEKNOFEST BNL MagLev competition chassis as a multi-part aluminium assembly (base plate, longitudinal rails, cross-member, bolted bracket set).

Envelope 400 x 60 x 30 mm; total chassis mass <= 0.35 kg; track guidance flatness <= 0.2 mm; track length <= 406 mm. Material Al 6061-T6 yield 276 MPa.

Load cases: LC1 steady 3.5 N longitudinal + 2.3 N lateral (allowable <= 138 MPa); LC2 25 N 10 ms impulse bump (allowable <= 184 MPa); LC3 launch 553 N hold-down.

Pass/fail: stress <= 138 MPa (LC1) / <= 184 MPa (LC2); first resonant frequency >= 40 Hz; FEM at assembled level; bolt pattern DCR <= 1.0.

prompt.title: national aerospace student benchmark + BNL maglev small-vehicle chassis (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part 6061-T6 maglev chassis: spine (A) + 2 magnet-carrier rails (B) + payload cradle (C) + 2 bumper stops (D) + tie-down fasteners (E). Overall 400x60x30 mm, 0.35 kg budget. Verified at assembly level: peak von Mises <= 138 MPa (LC1) / 184 MPa (LC2, LC3), first six modes >= 40 Hz, rail-channel flatness deviation <= 0.2 mm under LC1, vehicle length <= 406 mm, connection DCR <= 1.0 at all rail/bumper/cradle interfaces.

parts.name: central_spine
parts.role: 400 mm long x 60 mm wide x 20 mm tall 6061-T6 billet spine; structural backbone carrying bumper longitudinal/lateral loads into the rails and payload cradle

parts.name: magnet_carrier_rail
parts.role: 400 mm long x 20 mm wide x 4 mm thick rail with 18 mm x 5 mm channel seating 20 N42 ceramic magnets at 20 mm pitch; confined to the underside of the spine with the two rails at 25 mm gap

parts.name: payload_cradle
parts.role: 100 mm long x 40 mm wide x 10 mm deep trough on top of the spine with 4 M4 (4.5 mm clearance) tie-down holes; carries 50 g payload + 553 N install-drop shock

parts.name: bumper_stop
parts.role: 30 mm wide x 15 mm tall x 4 mm thick vertical plate at the front and rear of the spine; carries 3.5 N longitudinal + 2.3 N lateral under LC1 and 25 N 10 ms impulse under LC2

parts.name: tie_down_and_interface_fastener
parts.role: M4 grade-8.8 steel tie-down screws for the 4-hole payload pattern plus optional stub screws that join rails/bumpers to the spine in a multipart build

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: vehicle coordinates: +x forward along track, +y starboard, +z up; origin at spine rear-bottom-center

boundary_conditions.magnet_rails: distributed levitation-reaction force along the rail channels (LC1); rigid during LC2/LC3
boundary_conditions.bumper_loads: 3.5 N longitudinal + 2.3 N lateral on the rear bumper (LC1); 25 N 10 ms impulse on rear bumper (LC2); inverted chassis constrained at both bumpers (LC3)
boundary_conditions.payload_drop: 553 N vertical on payload cradle under LC3 with elastomer-pad stiffness BC
boundary_conditions.rail_spine_interface: bonded contact or stub-screw tie between rails and spine (IF1)
boundary_conditions.bumper_spine_interface: bonded contact or stub-screw tie between bumper plates and spine (IF2)
boundary_conditions.cradle_spine_interface: bonded contact or integral pocket between payload cradle and spine (IF3)

prompt.objective: primary: pass all six R-criteria under assembled_only FEM check. secondary: minimize assembly mass below 0.35 kg.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: rail-spine, bumper-spine, and cradle-spine interface demand-to-capacity per either bonded tie-surface allowable or M4 grade-8.8 bolt capacity
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

interfaces.load_path: rail-to-spine tie on the underside; transfers levitation-reaction load (LC1) and rail-channel flatness requirement back into the spine
bumper-to-spine tie at both ends; transfers bumper longitudinal/lateral and 25 N impulse load
cradle-to-spine tie on the top face; transfers 553 N install-drop shock into the spine
4 M4 tie-down bolts holding the payload into the cradle; carry 2.3 N lateral + 553 N vertical payload retention

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Although the original prompt calls out billet-machined monolithic construction, the chassis is functionally composed of distinct sub-parts (spine, rails, cradle, bumpers) each with their own interface load paths; the multipart sidecar exposes those interfaces without altering the allowable monolithic manufacturing path.
notes.speculation_caveats: Inter-part fastener count/type for IF1-IF3 is a speculation placeholder because the original sample treats the chassis as one billet; fasteners E are modeled explicitly only for IF4 (tie-downs, which are called out in the original).

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
